data.title: AAPL vs. MSFT vs. TSLA: Which Tech Stock Is Wall Street Favoring ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Technology stocks Tesla (TSLA), Microsoft (MSFT), and Apple (AAPL) are set to report earnings next week, between January 28 and January 30. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we looked at which of the three stocks Wall Street favors ahead of earnings. Microsoft stands out as the top pick, with analysts forecasting about 34% upside from current levels. Apple ranks second, with roughly 20% upside, pointing to steady but more cautious optimism. Tesla trails the group, as analysts see around 11% downside from current levels.

data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, Jan. 25
data.description:
U.S. stocks finished the week mixed as gains in technology shares helped offset weakness in financial stocks and rising rate pressure. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) fell 0.58% to $49,098.71, the S&P 500 (SPX) ended nearly flat, up 0.03% to $6,915.61, and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) outperformed, rising 0.34% to $25,605.47.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, interest rates continued to move higher. The 10-year Treasury yield climbed to about 4.23%, reflecting steady inflation concerns and caution ahead of earnings. In commodities, oil (CL) gained 2.78% to $61.24 per barrel as energy demand expectations firmed, while gold (XAUUSD) rose 0.7% to $4,982.50 as investors balanced risk exposure. Bitcoin (BTC) slipped 0.61% to $89,551 as digital assets paused after recent gains.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: 'Stay Away': Investors Sound the Alarm on XRP
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) was one of the biggest performers in the crypto rally that followed the 2024 U.S. election, surging more than 540% over roughly six weeks as investors bet on a friendlier regulatory climate and a potential resolution of the SEC's long-running lawsuit against Ripple over alleged unregistered securities sales.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Indeed, those catalysts played out last year, but since the court case was settled, XRP has given back a big chunk of those gains and now sits 48% below last year's highs.

For those hoping the depressed price action signals opportunity, some investors have recently been quick to pour cold water on that notion.

For example, investor Emma Newbery points to two glaring issues with the investment case.

One revolves around the tokenomics. When Ripple launched XRP, it created 100 billion tokens, locking about 34 billion in escrow. With 66 billion circulating, Ripple holds enough to influence the price. Moreover, unlike Bitcoin, which is capped at just $21 million coins, scarcity is unlikely to drive value.

Secondly, Newbery believes XRP's "use case is questionable." Ripple seems to be moving away from its original goal of providing low-cost cross-border payments. CEO Brad Garlinghouse recently told CNBC that the company is acquiring traditional finance firms to integrate its crypto solutions. While this may be a smart strategy for Ripple, it does little for XRP itself.

XRP's main role is as a bridge currency in Ripple's payment network, but that position is increasingly under pressure. Swift, the long-standing international payments network, is developing its own blockchain-based solutions, and other financial firms are exploring similar technologies. At the same time, stablecoins allow fast, low-cost international transfers without the price volatility of XRP, making the token less essential.

As such, Newbery thinks the outlook for XRP is "not as rosy as bulls might suggest."

Also homing in on the use case issue, investor Johnny Rice thinks there's an "important distinction investors need to understand between XRP itself and related products."

When people discuss adoption, says Rice, they're usually referring to the uptake of Ripple's financial products, not XRP itself. Banks can use these services without holding – or even interacting with – XRP.

RippleNet, the company's flagship product, illustrates this. Essentially a messaging platform, it competes with the traditional SWIFT system by enabling faster, cheaper settlements. While XRP can be used within RippleNet, most banks stick to fiat currencies, taking advantage of the network's technology rather than the token.

At the same time, even after the big pullback, Rice thinks XRP's valuation is hard to justify. Unlike stocks, cryptocurrencies don't have traditional metrics like earnings, cash flow, or assets to analyze.

One imperfect – but telling – comparison is Visa. The payment network has a ~$622 billion market cap, less than 5.5 times XRP's $116 billion, yet it handles roughly 640 times more transactions. While it's not a direct "apples-to-apples comparison," it highlights the stark gap between XRP's market value and the scale of actual transaction activity.

"At the end of the day," Rice summed up, "I believe XRP's current value is built on hype and speculation. That means it could reverse course and spike above $2.50 or higher in the near term, but it won't hold that value over the long term, and I would not own XRP." (To watch Rice's track record, click here)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Meta or Netflix: Cathie Wood Cuts Back on One Top Tech Stock While Doubling Down on Another
data.description:
Whether you're a fan of her investing style or not, there's no denying Cathie Wood does things her own way. The Ark Invest CEO tends to make big, high-conviction bets on disruptive technologies and is far more comfortable with volatility than most traditional fund managers. That focus on bold ideas is what makes her polarizing – Wood tends to draw plenty of criticism when markets turn against her, and renewed enthusiasm when those same themes come back into favor.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wood's predilection for eye-catching predictions was on display again this week. In Ark Invest's Big Ideas 2026 report, the firm forecasts that Bitcoin's market cap will rise to $16 trillion by 2030, suggesting each coin will be worth roughly $800,000, an ~800% jump from current levels.

As for the more day-to-day matter of portfolio management, Wood recently made moves involving two recent underperformers. Both Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) and Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) have struggled in recent months, but it appears that Wood thinks one of these names is worth considering at the moment, while the other has been receiving the trimming treatment. 

So, we've given these market giants a closer look to find out why one seems more appealing to Wood than the other right now. Let's dive in.

Meta Platforms

First up, Meta Platforms, the social media behemoth owner of Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. With billions of users across its family of apps and products that are deeply embedded in daily life, the company is known worldwide. Its scale, reach, and data footprint have long made it one of the most influential companies on the global scene, with the ability to even influence election outcomes. Additionally, it is an expert in monetizing attention through targeted advertising, which is still a core element of its business.

But not all Meta touches turn to gold. Famously, the Mark Zuckerberg-led company went all in on the metaverse, once considered the future of the internet, rebranding the company in the process and spending billions on virtual-reality research and the development of hardware, software, and 3D virtual environments. However, that failed to catch on, and the company decided to turn its attention in recent years to where all tech roads lead – AI.

But here, at the moment at least, Meta also seems to be running into problems. At present, Meta is widely seen as trailing rivals such as OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic in the AI race, despite spending heavily. Much of that perception comes from the fact Meta has yet to produce an AI product that clearly sets it apart and delivers real profitability, while it has a tendency to move fast and address problems later, a strategy that has resulted in quality issues.

Maybe Wood is waiting for Meta to get its AI game sorted before getting bullish again. This month, via the ARKF, ARKK, and ARKW funds, she sold $20.8 million worth of META shares.

Needham's Laura Martin echoes that caution. The analyst believes the company's AI struggles are likely to continue, posing a problem for shareholders.

"Meta investors seem to have growing investment risks owing to its small size, strategy clutter, and lack of incremental revenue streams to offset a portion of its GenAI capital spending. We believe META's risk/reward for public shareholders is deteriorating as Meta uses 100% of its FCF plus takes on debt and off-balance sheet obligations, without a clear path to positive returns on growing levels of capital intensity," Martin explained. "The GenAI battle is among giants, and META's size and debt capacity are far smaller than GOOGL and AMZN. To fund its CapX investments, press reports say META is structuring off-balance sheet liabilities via SPVs, which are senior obligations to equity value and, therefore, represent a headwind to share price appreciation, we believe."

Accordingly, Martin rates META shares a Hold (i.e., Neutral) without having a fixed price target in mind. (To watch Martin's track record, click here)

5 other analysts share that neutral view. On the bearish end of the spectrum, one analyst remains negative, but that lone Sell is heavily outnumbered by 37 Buy ratings. Altogether, this mix translates into a Strong Buy consensus rating. Based on the Street's average price target of $820.21, Meta is projected to deliver 24.5% upside over the next 12 months. (See Meta stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: XOM or CVX: Which Energy Stock Does Wall Street Favor Ahead of Q2 Earnings?
data.description:
Oil and gas giants Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) are scheduled to announce their results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on January 30. Lower commodity prices are expected to weigh on the Q4 results of energy companies. That said, several analysts remain optimistic about the solid execution and long-term growth prospects of Exxon Mobil and Chevron. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we placed XOM and CVX against each other to find the better pick, according to Wall Street.

data.title: QBTS Stock: Needham's Top Analyst Says, 'D-Wave Gains Edge via Circuits Buy'
data.description:
Needham's top analyst, Quinn Bolton,remains highly optimistic about D-Wave Quantum's (QBTS) competitive edge in the quantum computing landscape following its acquisition of Quantum Circuits (aka QCI). He reiterated his Buy rating and Street-high price target of $48, implying nearly 75% upside potential from current levels. Bolton awaits forthcoming qubit metrics and earnings results to update his model projections.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bolton is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #39 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 63% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 39.10%.

Here's What Happened

On January 20, D-Wave completed its acquisition of Quantum Circuits, reinforcing a dual-platform strategy that spans annealing and gate-model quantum systems. This move expands the firm's total addressable market (TAM) in quantum computing.

Bolton highlighted that QCI contributes superconducting gate-model systems with integrated error detection, which complements D-Wave's existing annealing technology. QCI's dual-rail qubit design offers fidelity comparable to ion traps, while leveraging superconducting gate speeds. This dual model allows for meaningful performance parity with leading gate-model rivals such as IBM (IBM), IonQ Inc. (IONQ), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), Google Quantum AI (GOOGL), and Quantinuum.  

Bolton anticipates QCI's dual-rail qubits, combined with D-Wave's cooling systems, to help build quantum computers with tens of thousands of qubits. He believes that this integration could reduce compatibility gaps between annealing and gate-model approaches. It could speed up building, software-hardware matching, and overall use by customers, adding to the stock's attractiveness.

Is D-Wave Stock a Good Investment?

Wall Street remains highly optimistic about D-Wave Quantum's long-term outlook. With 13 unanimous Buy ratings, QBTS stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. The average D-Wave Quantum price target of $39.75 implies 44.9% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, QBTS stock has skyrocketed over 354%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Top Analyst Upgrades Rigetti Stock (RGTI) to Buy on India's $8.4M Order
data.description:
B. Riley Securities' top analyst, Craig Ellis, upgraded Rigetti Computing (RGTI) to a Buy rating from Hold, while keeping his $35 price target (40.2% upside). He cited Rigetti's $8.4 million order from Indian firm C-DAC, which "enhances full-year estimate attainment visibility." Alongside Ellis, two other analysts reiterated their Buy ratings, highlighting Rigetti's expanding global footprint as a key growth driver. These ratings pushed RGTI shares up 5.5% on January 22.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ellis is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #100 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 56% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 31.60%.

India Order a Big Boost for Rigetti's Sales

On January 20, Rigetti announced that its Indian subsidiary received an $8.4 million purchase order to deliver a 108-qubit quantum computer to the Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. The system will be installed on-premises at C-DAC's Bengaluru center and is slated for deployment in the second half of 2026.

The order's value has been a big catalyst for analysts' enthusiasm since Rigetti reported total revenues of $7.49 million over the past twelve months. Ellis pointed out that the $8.4 million order represents 48% of the low-end and 36% of the high-end consensus revenue estimates for calendar year 2025.

Analysts Offer Bullish Views on RGTI

Rosenblatt Securities analyst John McPeake reiterated his Buy rating and $40 price target, implying 60.3% upside potential. He emphasized that the India order strengthens Rigetti's already robust installed base and validates sustained demand for its on-premises superconducting quantum solutions.

Additionally, Craig-Hallum analyst Richard Shannon reiterated his Buy rating, echoing similar catalysts and validating Rigetti's long-term outlook. While the India order is a major win, Rigetti faces certain risks including execution delays, funding constraints, and competitive pressure from IonQ (IONQ), IBM (IBM), and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS).

Is Rigetti a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Rigetti Computing stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Rigetti Computing price target of $39.75 implies 59.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, RGTI shares have surged over 85%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Top Stifel Analyst Raises Alphabet Stock (GOOGL) Price Target Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Google-parent Alphabet (GOOGL) is scheduled to announce its fourth-quarter results on February 4. Ahead of the results, top Stifel analyst Mark Kelley reiterated a Buy rating on GOOGL stock and raised the price target to $346 from $333, citing favorable insights from recent checks. GOOGL stock has risen about 66% over the past year, driven by optimism about the company's Gemini 3 AI model, attractive offerings like AI Overviews in Search, and the strength in Google Cloud.

data.title: KeyBanc Remains Cautious on Apple Stock (AAPL) Ahead of Q1 Earnings
data.description:
Tech giant Apple (AAPL) is scheduled to announce its results for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 on January 29. Ahead of Q1 FY26 earnings, KeyBanc analyst Brandon Nispel reiterated a Hold rating on Apple stock, saying that he continues to have certain concerns about the iPhone maker. Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Apple to report earnings per share (EPS) of $2.67 for Q1 FY26, reflecting an 11.3% year-over-year growth. Revenue is also estimated to rise 11.3% to $138.38 billion.
